name: Cream of Mushroom Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds white cultivated mushrooms, thinly sliced
salt and freshly ground black pepper
3 1/2 cups low-salt chicken broth, reserving 1/2 cup
ground nutmeg to taste -- about 1/4 teaspoon
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, melt butter with olive oil over moderate heat.
2. Add the shallots and cook until tender, about 2 to 3 minutes.
3. Add the sliced mushrooms, thyme leaves, salt, and pepper; cook until all liquid from the mushrooms has evaporated.
4. Add the cream sherry and cook until it evaporates.
5. Remove half of the cooked mushrooms and set aside.
6. Stir in the flour and cook for 2 to 3 minutes.
7. Add 3 cups of chicken broth, bring to a simmer, and cook for 15 minutes.
8. In a blender, puree the soup in batches until smooth. For an ultra-smooth soup, pass through a fine sieve.
9. Return the soup to the saucepan, bring to a simmer, and whisk in the milk.
10. Add the reserved chicken broth to achieve the desired consistency. Adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
11. Lower heat and whisk in 1/4 cup of sour cream. Keep the soup warm but do not simmer or boil.
12. Serve garnished with the reserved cooked mushrooms and a dollop of the remaining sour cream.
Yield: 4 servings
